fields.helpText: An employee name has the following order and meaning: LAST NAME, followed by a comma; FIRST NAME or INITIAL; a blank space; MIDDLE NAME or INITIAL; and if applicable, a blank space, JR, SR or INITIAL. For example, DOE,JOHN T JR. No periods are placed after either initials or titles.

Code indicates the amount of additional basic life insurance applicable to projectable rotating or permanent tours of duty at night rates for shift 2 or 3. Applies to hourly rate Wage or Veteran Canteen Service employees.
Station entered-on-duty date.

Identifies the specialty assignment of physicians, dentists and nurses; the religious denomination of chaplains; summer employees, trainees and other special programs.  Must be used with both the occupation series code and the occupation series title.
Field indicates an employee's United States citizenship status.
An employee's major field of study.
The number assigned to a Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Installation for identification and control purposes.
The number identifies a specific group of employees for time and leave tracking purposes.
The number used for identification and control purposes to refer to an employee.
Code identifies the time basis that an employee is scheduled to work.  An employee may have a full-time, part-time or intermittent basis work schedule.
Field identifies an employee's educational attainment. Code is based on years of schooling and academic degrees conferred.
Code identifies whether an employee is covered under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) minimum wage and overtime provisions.
Code identifies the primary work function of the position.  Applies to scientist and engineering positions.
An indicator of hierarchal relationships among positions covered by the same pay plan or system.
Indicates an employee's eligibility for leave.  Also indicates the basis under a leave system for determining the rate of leave accrual.
The number of hours an employee is hired to work each pay period. Hours are established or changed by an official personnel action.
Code that identifies the subdivision of an occupational family or group under which an employee's position is classified.
Code that identifies the subdivision of an occupational family or group under which an employee's position is classified.
Identifies the Cost Center for fund appropriation accounting.  This is related to the first of four possible Labor Distribution codes.
Identifies a type of cost expenditure such as one of the personal service codes, one of the travel and transportation codes, etc., used for cost accounting purposes.
Code identifies the principal condition in terms of time, production or other criteria that serve as a basis for computing pay.
Code identifies the pay system under which the employee's compensation is determined.
Identifies special factors that were considered in determining an employee's salary.
The code used to identify an employee's level of performance/proficiency.

The number assigned to an employee's position under a pay system which requires a position number.  The last character in the number indicates if the position can be filled by more than one employee and if it is a permanent or temporary position.
Code identifies an assigned level of achievement for an employee's accomplishment of assigned tasks for the past rating period.
Code identifies an assigned level of achievement for an employee's accomplishment of assigned tasks for the second past rating period.
Identifies the retirement system and type of retirement that covers an employee.
The salary date indicates the beginning of the employee's waiting period for the next within-grade increase.
Rate of compensation which is payable to an employee. The rate is used in computing salary payments. The rate does not include night differential or other premium pay.
The rate of compensation shown on the salary schedule for an employee's grade which the employee would receive if there was no legislative cap on the amount which can be paid.

Service Computation Date (SCD) is the date computed based on the employee's prior Federal civilian and military service which is used to determine  benefits which are based on how long the person has been in the Federal service.
Indicates whether an individual is male or female.
The month, day and year an employee was born.
Indicates the employee's actual social security number when a dummy number was assigned for the social security number because the employee has two appointments at the same facility.

This is the social security number that replaces the previous number. The previous number may have been invalid or a temporarily assigned number while the employee applied for a social security number.

Is used to identify an employee fitting one of the following conditions: dual appointment (2 master records); dual appropriation (1 master record, two different jobs); corporate employer identification number (EIN). This field is currently inactive.
Identifies the station number where an employee's official personnel folder is maintained.

Identifies positions in a competitive area that are the same grade or occupational level and which are so similar that employees may be assigned from one position to another during a reduction in force (RIF).
An indicator of a specific salary within a specific grade or level pay band.
Indicates if an employee holds a supervisory position and, if so, the level of supervision.
Code indicates that a temporary employee has elected health insurance and that deductions are to be taken for both the employee's share and the government's share of the premium.
Code identifies the employee as a supervisor, work leader, general foreman, foreman or helper.

Code identifies the type of appointment (Career Conditional, Career, etc.) under which an employee is serving and indicates whether the appointment is in the competitive service, excepted service, or Senior Executive Service (SES). The code also indicates whether the appointment is permanent or temporary, and what tenure group applies.
Code identifies categories of entitlement to preference in the federal service based on active military service that terminated honorably.
Indicates whether an employee is eligible for a Within Grade Increase (WGI) or whether the WGI is withheld.
Indicates that the Within Grade Increase's SF50 has been issued three pay periods in advance of the proposed due date.

The status of the employee's position regarding Federal employee bargaining unit representation.
Indicates that the position occupied by the employee is centralized to the Secretary of the Department of Veterans Affairs or other VA Central Office official.
Indicates an employee is working a tour that is approved as a compressed or flextime schedule. The field can be blank or coded with a "C" for Compressed or "F" for flextime.
The amount of military service (years and months) which has been used to compute the employee's service computation date (SCD) for leave.

The legal authority assigned by law, executive order, rule, regulation or other basis that authorizes the appointment of the employee to his/her current position.  At times two current appointment authority codes may be authorized: CURRENT APPT AUTH 1 and CURRENT APPT AUTH 2.

The legal authority assigned by law, executive order, rule, regulation or other basis that authorizes the appointment of the employee to his/her current position.  At times two current appointment authority codes may be authorized: CURRENT APPT AUTH 1 and CURRENT APPT AUTH 2.

The total years and months of civilian and military service creditable for calculation of Service Computation Date (Leave) at the time the employee first becomes covered by the CSRS Offset or Federal Employees Retirement System (FERS).
Indicates that an employee was not paid in a particular month and should not be included in certain employment reports. Value: N - Include; Y - Exclude.
Code which identifies an employee who currently participates in or has participated in the LVA Program.  Used for tracking purposes.
An employee's medical specialty occupation while serving in the military.
Identifies an employee who is a retired member of a military service.  Also indicates whether the employee's retirement pay is subject to reduction.
The number of hours or days an employee is not being paid because he/she was not an employee in the last pay period processed.
Indicates that an employee's net pay is not paid through the Central PAID System.

Indicates employee is an Office of Workman's Compensation Program beneficiary. Leave without pay charged will not be used for determining within grade increase eligibility or adjustment of service computation date.

Code indicates that an employee's actual hours worked must be accumulated. "C" is placed here if Retirement/FICA = 1 or 4 and not reemployed annuitant and Pay Plan = L,M,N or Q and Duty Basis = 2 or 3 and Pay Basis is not "S".

Indicates employee's appointment is subject to completion of a probationary or trail period before it is considered permanent.

Code indicates that a deceased employee has survivors eligible to receive retirement annuity payments.
Indicates that the employee's appointment carries a restriction on either the number of hours of work which may be performed for pay purposes or on total salary dollars which may be earned.
This is the beginning date of a salary limitation appointment.
Maximum number of hours or maximum salary dollars a part-time or intermittent employee is authorized to work in a year.  Limitation is based on the anniversary date of appointment.

The accumulated number of hours worked by an employee from the date of appointment through the last pay period processed. This total is applied against the pay status hours limit established for a part-time or intermittent employee. On the anniversary date of the appointment this field is initialized in preparation for accumulating hours for the next year.
Identifies a separated employee.  Separated employees have a value of Y.  Current employees have a value of N.

The dollar and cents amount the employee receives as special pay.

Code identifies the second geographic preference for mobility assignment purposes of incumbents or candidates for centralized Title 38 physician, dentist, or nurse positions.
Position for which a candidate or incumbent wishes to be considered when a vacancy occurs.

The second line of the address, selected by the employee, to which his/her biweekly salary checks are to be mailed.

The amount of additional primary city income tax that an employee has authorized to be withheld from earnings each pay period.
The amount of primary city income tax withheld from an employee's gross earnings in the last pay period processed.

The accumulated amount withheld from an employee's earnings for the primary city income tax in the current tax period to date. Tax periods are determined by the employee's city as biweekly, monthly or quarterly. This amount includes any additional withholding amount and the withholding based on claimed allowances

The accumulated amount of primary city income tax withheld from an employee's earnings for the current tax year to date. This amount includes any additional withholding amount and the withholding based on claimed allowances.

The code represents the number of exemptions; and/or any special factors that affect the computation of tax withholding from an employee's taxable earnings. The precise meaning of the code is determined by income tax regulations of an employee's primary city.

The code represents the number of exemptions; and/or any special factors that affect the computation of tax withholdings from an employee's taxable earnings. The precise meaning of the code is determined by income tax regulations of an employee's primary city.

The accumulated amount of an employee's gross earnings that are subject to primary city tax in the current tax year to date. Tax periods are determined by the employee's primary city as biweekly, monthly or quarterly.
The accumulated amount of an employee's gross earnings that are subject to income tax in the primary city for the current tax year to date.
Code identifies the assigned primary city tax withholding GSA code which indicates where payment or information will be reported to the taxing authority.
This code is used to indicate an employee's marital status, married or single, for the purpose of computing primary city tax. Marital status is not a factor for every city tax.
Code indicates whether an employee is a resident of the primary city in which taxes are being withheld.
The amount of additional secondary city income tax that an employee has authorized to be withheld from earnings each pay period.

The accumulated amount withheld from an employee's earnings for the secondary city income tax in the current tax period to date. Tax periods are determined by the employee's city as biweekly, monthly or quarterly. This amount includes any additional withholding amount and the withholding based on claimed allowances.

The accumulated amount of secondary city income tax withheld from an employee's earnings for the current tax year to date. This amount includes any additional withholding amount and the withholding based on claimed allowances.
The amount of secondary city income tax withheld from an employee's gross earnings in the current pay period.

Code represents the number of exemptions; and/or any special factors that affect the computation of tax withholdings from an employee's taxable earnings. The precise meaning of the code is determined by income tax regulations of an employee's secondary city.

Code represents the number of exemptions; and/or any special factors that affect the computation of tax withholdings from an employee's taxable earnings. The precise meaning of the code is determined by income tax regulations of an employee's secondary city.
