fields.helpText: Regular hours at the overtime rate for week-1.

fields.fieldName: REG HOURS AT OT RATE WK 2
fields.helpText: Regular hours at the overtime rate for week-2.

fields.fieldName: REG HOURS AT OT RATE AMT WK 1
fields.helpText: Regular hours at the overtime rate amount for week-1.

fields.fieldName: REG HOURS AT OT RATE AMT WK 2
fields.helpText: Regular hours at the overtime rate amount for week-2.
Non-overtime hours.
Non-overtime amount.
Overtime hours on a holiday.
Overtime amount on a holiday.
Training hours for week-1.
Training hours for week-2.
Travel hours for week-1.
Travel hours for week-2.
Travel overtime hours for week-1.
Travel overtime hours for week-2.
MI Review Group for education tracking.
This multiple field contains all the mandatory classes for an individual.
This is a single character code that identifies the type of telework agreement the employee is eligible to work.
This field is a pointer to the NEW PERSON (#200) file.

A discretionary component of Title 38 special pay authorized for service in a Geographic Location that has extraordinary problems in recruiting or retaining physicians or dentists in certain specialties.
A discretionary component of Title 38 special pay that may be authorized for a physician or dentist with special or distinguishing qualifications within a specialty or category of positions.
A discretionary component of Title 38 special pay that is authorized for physicians and dentists in certain positions.  This component is also known as Executive Position Pay.
A discretionary component of Title 38 special pay that may by authorized for a physician or dentist with a scarce medical or dental specialty.
A VA approved maximum amount of total salary (base pay, GAP if applicable, and special pay) a physician or dentist may receive above the EL I pay cap.
The first amount deducted for purposes of tax garnishment.
The second amount deducted for purposes of tax garnishment.
The first amount deducted for purposes of commerical garnishment.
The second amount deducted for purposes of commercial garnishment.
The number of hours or days used by an employye for care and bereavement leave in the last pay period processed.
The total number of hours or days of care and bereavement leave used by an employee during the leave year to date.
The number of hours or days used by an employee for adoption leave in the last pay period processed.
The total number of hours or days of adoption leave used by an employee during the leave year to date.
The number of hours or days used by an employee for other sick leave in the last pay period processed.
The total number of hours or days of other sick leave used by an employee during the leave year to date.
The number of hours or days used by an employee for donor leave in the last pay period processed.
The total number of hours or days of donor leave used by an employee during the leave year to date.
The one character string that identifies the type of the first bond.
The one character string that identifies the type of the second bond.
The first line of the address, selected by the employee, to which the third bond is mailed.
The second line of the address, selected by the employee, to which the third bond is mailed.
The third line of the address, selected by the employee, to which the third bond is mailed.

The accumulated amount deducted toward the purchase price of the third bond, as of the current pay period. Each time the purchase price is reached, a bond is issued and the balance adjusted.  Remaining balances are incorporated in future bond purchases. The balance is refunded if the bond is canceled or the employee separates from the agency.
Code indicates whether or not an employee has selected a beneficiary or co-owner for a bond purchased through the payroll deduction plan.  This field pertains to the third bond allotment.
The name of the designated beneficiary/co-owner of the third bond. A beneficiary or co-owner must be a person.
The social security number of the beneficiary or co-owner designated by the employee. This field pertains to the third bond allotment.

The balance is the accumulated amount deducted toward the purchase price of the bond, as of the current pay period. Each time the purchase price is reached, a bond is issued and the balance adjusted. Remaining balances are incorporated into future bond purchases. The balance is refunded if the bond is canceled or the employee separates from the agency. This field pertains to the third bond allotment.
The amount the employee has authorized to be deducted each pay period for the purchase of a U.S. savings bond. This field pertains to the third savings bond allotment.
Code indicates the face value of the U.S. savings bond being purchased through the payroll deduction plan. An employee may purchase more than one bond at a time. This field represents the third bond.

Denotes the date when the third bond allotment deductions have reached one-half of the purchase price of the bond. The effective date determines the bond's maturity and when interest begins to accrue.

The name of the designated owner of a U.S. savings bond purchased by an employee through the payroll deduction plan. The owner may be the employee, another individual, a public or private organization, or a fiduciary. This field pertains to the third bond.

The social security number of the designated owner of a U.S. savings bond purchased by an employee through the payroll deduction plan. If the owner is not a person, an employer identification number must be shown. This field reflects the owner's social security number for the third bond.
The zip code of the address, selected by the employee, to which the third bond is mailed.
The one character string that identifies the type of the third bond.
The first line of the address, selected by the employee, to which the fourth bond is mailed.
The second line of the address, selected by the employee, to which the fourth bond is mailed.
The third line of the address, selected by the employee, to which the fourth bond is mailed.

The accumulated amount deducted toward the purchase price of the fourth bond, as of the current pay period. Each time the purchase price is reached, a bond is issued and the balance adjusted.  Remaining balances are incorporated in future bond purchases. The balance is refunded if the bond is canceled or the employee separates from the agency.
Code indicates whether or not an employee has selected a beneficiary or co-owner for a bond purchased through the payroll deduction plan.  This field pertains to the fourth bond allotment.
The name of the designated beneficiary/co-owner of the fourth bond. A beneficiary or co-owner must be a person.
The social security number of the beneficiary or co-owner designated by the employee. This field pertains to the fourth bond allotment.

The balance is the accumulated amount deducted toward the purchase price of the bond, as of the current pay period. Each time the purchase price is reached, a bond is issued and the balance adjusted. Remaining balances are incorporated into future bond purchases. The balance is refunded if the bond is canceled or the employee separates from the agency. This field pertains to the fourth bond allotment.
The amount the employee has authorized to be deducted each pay period for the purchase of a U.S. savings bond. This field pertains to the fourth savings bond allotment.

Code indicates the face value of the U.S. savings bond being purchased through the payroll deduction plan. An employee may purchase more than one bond at a time. This field represents the fourth bond.

Denotes the date when the fourth bond allotment deductions have reached one-half of the purchase price of the bond. The effective date determines the bond's maturity and when interest begins to accrue.

The name of the designated owner of a U.S. savings bond purchased by an employee through the payroll deduction plan. The owner may be the employee, another individual, a public or private organization, or a fiduciary. This field pertains to the fourth bond.

The social security number of the designated owner of a U.S. savings bond purchased by an employee through the payroll deduction plan. If the owner is not a person, an employer identification number must be shown. This field reflects the owner's social security number for the fourth bond.
The zip code of the address, selected by the employee, to which the fourth bond is mailed.
The one character string that identifies the type of the fourth bond.
Indicates whether the employee is eligible to participate in the Federal Long Term Care Insurance Program (FLTCIP).  Possible values are Y or N.
The amount to be deducted each pay period from an employee's pay for Federal Long Term Care Insurance Program.

fields.fieldName: FED EMP HLTH BENE CRT ORD IND
fields.helpText: The Federal Employee Health Benefit Court Order Indicator identifies employees who are under court order to pay for Alimony, Child Support or Bankruptcy.  Possible values are C (under court order) or blank (not under court order).

The Earnings and Leave Print Indicator determines whether or not to print a hardcopy of the employee's Earnings and Leave statement. Possible values are X (print a hardcopy) or blank (don't print a hardcopy).
When set to Y this field will prevent the employee from making changes via EEX to their federal tax withholding amount.  (Possible Values: Y or N)
The type of interface or the internal entry number of the last person who changed any of the fields related to the Labor Distribution codes assigned to the employee.
The type of interface from Central PAID that changed the employee's Labor Distribution codes.  Possible values:   1 - INITIAL 2 - EDIT & UPDATE 3 - TRANSFER 4 - PAYRUN
The date and time when the employee's Labor Distribution codes were last changed.
